meaning: v.驥肴眠隶ｾ隶｡n.驥肴眠隶ｾ隶｡,譁ｰ隶ｾ隶｡
examples: We need to redesign the school system so that it produces a well-educated population.
Publisher Gregory Coleman said he expected the redesign to boost advertising sales.
It would most likely cause insurers to redesign plans to fall beneath the threshold.
Wardrop also leans on his suppliers to redesign any failed components rather than simply replace them.
Smith's ground-hog revamp is the most radical redesign of FedEx in its 28-year history.
